title: micropolypodium sikkimense
intro: Micropolypodium sikkimense, also known as Himalayan polymodium, is a type of fern native to the Himalayan region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which are believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and improving digestion. Traditional herbal preparations include making teas, infusions, and tonics from the plant's leaves, which are also used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine. The health benefits of Micropolypodium sikkimense are attributed to its ability to modulate the immune system, reduce oxidative stress, and exhibit antimicrobial properties.
